_comment: gate-protect.py is bundled ALWAYS-ON (a PreToolUse deny on Write|Edit to the .factory/ verifier substrate) — the keystone enforcement, in force in every session with the plugin enabled. SCOPE, stated honestly: this is a Write|Edit matcher, so it mechanically denies the EXECUTOR (the app-worker — Read/Grep/Glob/Edit/Write, NO Bash) from forging a pass signal, laundering the ledger, or editing the sealed bar it is graded against, while leaving its real work writable (specs, build/, the bar SOURCE in spec/bars/). It does NOT intercept Bash, so the independent Bash-carrying critics (app-validator/app-refuter/app-distiller) are NOT gated by this hook — their integrity rests on role-separation (a family disjoint from the worker, fresh isolated context, the build read as untrusted data) and the validate path (signals minted only by validate.py from a verifier's exit status), not on this gate. The mechanical gate binds the one role with an incentive to hack its own grade; the critics are trusted by construction. harness-hook stays advisory (PostToolUse, never blocks).
